questions.q: Which Italian Renaissance artist sculpted the famous statue of David?
Which famous Italian city is widely considered the birthplace of the Renaissance?
Which Italian banking family famously patronized Renaissance artists in Florence?
Which Italian artist painted The Last Supper, on the wall of a Milanese refectory?
Michelangelo painted what masterpiece on the ceiling of a Vatican chapel?
Which famous Renaissance painting depicts Adam reaching out to receive life from God?
In Renaissance fashion, what was the term for slashing outer fabric to reveal the inner layer?
What is a 'putto' in Renaissance art?
choices: A naked cherub or baby angel

questions.q: Which Renaissance artist sculpted the famous work 'Judith and Holofernes'?
Which famous Italian poet wrote 'The Divine Comedy' during the early Renaissance period?
Raphael painted the 'Portrait of Baldassare Castiglione,' a work depicting what type of person?
Which artist painted 'The Venus of Urbino,' one of the most famous female nudes of the Renaissance?
Which Florentine architect is credited with perfecting single-point perspective in art?
Paracelsus made major contributions to the Renaissance study of what field?
In what year was Miguel de Cervantes' 'Don Quixote' first published during the Renaissance?
Which Dutch painter created 'The Garden of Earthly Delights,' a surreal Renaissance masterpiece?
Duchess, Princess, Rosepoint, and Renaissance are all types of what textile craft?
Which Renaissance monarch of England broke with the Roman Catholic Church?
Paolo Veronese is famous for painting which work depicting a biblical banquet scene?
choices: The Feeding of the Five Thousand

questions.q: Michelangelo sculpted the famous marble statue of 'David' during which century?
Which Flemish painter created 'Ill-Matched Lovers,' a satirical Renaissance work?
What 19th-century movement for the unification of Italy is known as the 'Risorgimento'?
Leonardo da Vinci's 'The Last Supper' was painted in which Italian city?
Which Renaissance philosopher and diplomat wrote 'The Prince,' a influential work on political power?
Which Renaissance artist is famous for painting the Mona Lisa?
What does the word 'Renaissance' literally mean?
What is the name of the famous art museum in Florence that houses Renaissance masterpieces?
Which Renaissance artist lived from 1452 to 1519 and was known for his diverse talents?
Who painted 'The Wedding at Cana' during the Renaissance?
Which nickname meaning 'The Divine One' was given to Michelangelo?
What famous epic poem did Dante Alighieri write during the Medieval-Renaissance transition?
Which artist painted 'Bacchus and Ariadne,' a famous Renaissance mythological scene?
Which Renaissance scholar wrote 'In Praise of Folly' (1511), satirizing church corruption?
Which Renaissance architect designed the Pazzi Chapel in Florence, pioneering the use of classical proportions and geometric harmony?
Machiavelli's 'The Prince' was written as a political treatise addressed to which member of the Medici family?
choices: Lorenzo II de' Medici, Duke of Urbino

questions.q: Which Medici pope commissioned Raphael to paint the Vatican's 'School of Athens' fresco?
Desiderius Erasmus' 'Praise of Folly' employs which literary device where a personified concept delivers the entire oration?
Which Renaissance anatomist's detailed illustrations challenged medieval medical doctrine and established modern anatomical study?
Sandro Botticelli's 'The Birth of Venus' was commissioned by which prominent Florentine family as an allegorical work?
Which Italian Renaissance poet's 'Orlando Furioso' became the archetypal work of chivalric romance literature?
In Renaissance art theory, what term describes the harmonious arrangement of figures in a pyramidal or triangular composition?
Which Spanish Renaissance painter, known for his elongated figures and spiritual intensity, eventually settled in Toledo?
Petrarch's rediscovery and championing of which Roman orator's writings helped launch the Renaissance humanist movement?
Which technique, involving the careful layering of thin glazes, did Netherlandish painters like Jan van Eyck perfect during the Northern Renaissance?
In Renaissance political theory, what concept proposed that a ruler's actions should be judged by practical results rather than moral principles?
Which Renaissance physician and alchemist famously rejected Galenic medicine and emphasized empirical observation and chemical remedies?
Michelangelo's 'Sistine Chapel ceiling' fresco depicts scenes from which biblical text as its primary subject matter?
Which Italian Renaissance city-state was governed by the Gonzaga family and became a major center of humanist learning and art?
Leonardo da Vinci's notebooks reveal his studies of human proportions, influenced by which Roman architectural treatise he deeply admired?
choices: Ten Books on Architecture by Alberti

questions.q: Which Renaissance architect designed the dome of Florence Cathedral, a feat of engineering that remained unmatched for centuries?
In Renaissance anatomical studies, which Italian artist and scientist produced detailed drawings based on actual human cadaver dissections?
The Medici family's patronage of the arts was partly motivated by their dominance in which financial industry during the Renaissance?
Which Renaissance sculptor created the bronze doors for the Florence Baptistry, pioneering linear perspective in relief sculpture?
In Renaissance music theory, which compositional technique involves the simultaneous combination of independent melodic lines?
Which Renaissance humanist scholar was chief librarian of the Vatican Library and compiled a vast collection of classical manuscripts?
The Renaissance technique of creating the illusion of depth on a flat surface through mathematical calculation is known as what?
Which Northern Renaissance artist created 'The Hunters in the Snow', a landscape painting distinguished by its attention to atmospheric detail?
In Renaissance Florence, which powerful banking family sponsored Botticelli's 'Primavera' as a celebration of their political influence?
Which Renaissance polymath developed a system of human proportions based on geometric principles, detailed in his treatise 'Four Books on Human Proportion'?
The Renaissance printing press invented by Gutenberg in approximately 1440 primarily accelerated the spread of which type of material?
Which Renaissance painter of Venice was renowned for his dramatic use of color and light in works like 'Assumption of the Virgin'?
In Renaissance courtly culture, which Italian writer authored 'The Book of the Courtier', a guide to ideal behavior and accomplishment?
Which Renaissance mathematical technique, developed by Filippo Brunelleschi, allowed artists to represent three-dimensional space using a vanishing point?
The Renaissance scholar Pico della Mirandola is best known for championing which intellectual movement that emphasized human dignity and potential?
Which Renaissance architect designed the Palazzo Farnese in Rome, exemplifying classical proportions and harmonic relationships?
What term, French for 'rebirth,' describes the period of cultural and artistic flourishing in 14th-17th century Europe?
Which Italian artist, nicknamed 'Il Divino,' painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling?
Which artist painted 'The Birth of Venus' featuring the goddess emerging from a shell?
Which 1517 event by Martin Luther sparked the Protestant Reformation?
Who painted 'The School of Athens,' a fresco of ancient philosophers in the Vatican?
Which Italian sculptor's bronze 'David' was the first freestanding nude bronze of the Renaissance?
Which Italian polymath designed inventions including a flying machine and a tank in his notebooks?
Which 16th-century astronomer proposed the heliocentric model in 'De revolutionibus orbium coelestium'?
Which 1455 invention by Gutenberg revolutionized European communication?
Which dome on Florence's cathedral, designed by Brunelleschi, is one of the great Renaissance engineering feats?
Which political treatise, written by Niccolò Machiavelli, describes power and manipulation in Renaissance governance?
In Renaissance Florence, the Medici family rose to prominence primarily through their success in which profession?
The Renaissance concept of 'humanism' primarily emphasized the study of which ancient texts?
In 1440, Johannes Gutenberg's invention of the movable-type printing press revolutionized the Renaissance by enabling what?
choices: Rapid distribution of books and ideas

questions.q: Leonardo da Vinci's 'Mona Lisa' is painted on which type of surface?
Which Renaissance artist designed the courtyard of the Palazzo Farnese in Rome?
In Renaissance Venice, which economic factor made the city a dominant power in Mediterranean trade?
Raphael's 'School of Athens' fresco, painted in the Vatican, depicts philosophers from which ancient civilization?
Which Renaissance king of England commissioned the English translation of the Bible known as the Great Bible?
The Renaissance technique of 'sfumato,' perfected by Leonardo da Vinci, refers to the use of which artistic element?
choices: Soft shading and gradual color transitions

questions.q: Which Renaissance cardinal sponsored the Complutensian Polyglot Bible, printing Hebrew, Greek, and Latin texts side by side?
Michelangelo's marble sculpture 'Pieta,' depicting the Virgin Mary mourning Jesus, was completed in which year?
Which Renaissance city served as the capital of the Papal States and became a major center of Renaissance art and architecture?
Raphael's 'The School of Athens' was commissioned to decorate which Vatican building?
Which Northern Renaissance painter is known for his detailed religious works and was active primarily in Bruges during the 15th century?
In Renaissance Florence, the Medici family's patronage of the arts was primarily motivated by which of the following?
Sandro Botticelli's 'The Birth of Venus' was likely inspired by which classical source?
Which Renaissance composer served as the maestro di cappella at St. Mark's Basilica in Venice and is known for his polyphonic choral works?
In Renaissance architecture, the term 'rustication' refers to which design element?
choices: The use of marble columns and capitals
Decorative painted murals on interior walls
Ornamental wooden beams in ceiling construction
Rough, deliberately textured stone facing on building exteriors

questions.q: The Renaissance concept of 'sprezzatura,' popularized in Baldassare Castiglione's writings, refers to which quality in courtly behavior?
choices: Rigorous adherence to strict social protocols
The appearance of effortless grace and nonchalance
Elaborate displays of wealth and status
